ip_outcome_note: Card name contains 'Announcer' explicitly. The World Tournament Announcer is canonically described as having blonde hair, sunglasses, and a navy-blue or black suit — matching the Vision description exactly (suited blond figure with sunglasses holding a microphone). The Announcer is one of the few Dragon Ball humans to appear across all major tournament arcs. The card's oracle text confirms 'Character: Announcer' and Special Trait 'Earthling/World Tournament'. Verified.

trivia_bullets.text: The World Tournament Announcer (Tenkaichi Budokai Anaunsaa) has hosted every World Martial Arts Tournament in the original Dragon Ball manga through Dragon Ball GT — making him one of the longest-running supporting characters in the franchise. He is one of the few normal humans fully aware that the Z fighters are superhuman and that Mr. Satan did not actually defeat Cell.
sources: WebSearch: Dragon Ball Wiki Fandom snippet on World Tournament Announcer
WebSearch: Behind The Voice Actors Dragon Ball Z Announcer page

trivia_bullets.text: Announcer, Ever-Curious (TB2-066) is a black-color Common Battle card (1 energy, 1,000 power, 5,000 combo). Its on-play ability: if the Leader is 'World Tournament,' look at the top 7 cards of the deck, choose up to 1 'World Tournament' card among them and add it to hand, then shuffle. This tutor effect makes the Announcer a consistency piece — fitting his narrative role as the person who controls the information flow of every tournament.
sources: WebSearch: DBZ Exchange TB2-066 product page

trivia_bullets.text: At 1,000 base power, the Announcer is the lowest-power battle card in this wave — he cannot meaningfully attack. But his top-7 tutor is strong in a dedicated World Tournament deck, fetching key leader-linked pieces on demand. The card's black color identity also separates him from the yellow cluster of fighters in TB2, which mirrors his role as an observer rather than a combatant.
sources: WebSearch: Big Orbit Cards TB2-066 listing

related_cards.name: I'm the World Champion!
related_cards.note: The Announcer knows Mr. Satan is a fraud; both are World Tournament-trait non-fighters in the same set.

related_cards.name: Risque Ranfan
related_cards.note: Announcer hosted the 21st Tenkaichi Budokai where Ranfan competed; both are tournament-arc supporting cast.

related_cards.name: Master Roshi, Martial Meister
related_cards.note: Roshi entered as Jackie Chun under the Announcer's watch at the 21st and 22nd tournaments; shared World Tournament trait.
